html:
Exclusive to:

GenericStumpSaplingSapling (Item)

Gloomthorns are one of two ancient trees that grow from a Surprising Seed. There are two requirements for it to grow: in Winter and is planted on Marsh Turf or Mud Turf.

To grow a Gloomthorn seed into a sapling, the seed must meet one of the conditions. If both of these conditions are met but the character says that the seed can't grow when examined, it means that it is the seed for a Sproutrock. Otherwise, the seed will grow into a Gloomthorn Sapling after 3-7 days.

Once the seed has sprouted into a Gloomthorn Sapling, it will only continue to grow if both of the conditions are met. If they are, it will turn into a Gloomthorn after 8-12 days.

Gloomthorns will grow 4 Nightberries every 12 to 25 days provided it is still on Marsh or Mud Turf. The Nightberries can only be picked at night.

The timer for Nightberry regrowth is set upon creation of the seed and will stay the same throughout its lifetime and every harvest. Chopping the tree and replanting the seed will not change this regrowth timer. The regrowth timer will be reduced by 25% in Spring, despite the produce being edible.

Gloomthorns and their saplings are completely fireproof and cannot be ignited by any means, including Wildfires.

Tips

  • Maxwell's Shadow Servants will not mine or harvest the Gloomthorns.

Gallery

  • Gloomthorn concept art from Staying Afloat update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • Gloomthorn concept art from Staying Afloat update stream from Rhymes with Play.

GenericStumpSaplingSapling (Item)
“Tree-t with caution.”
–Wilson, Generic
“What an ugly tree.”
–Willow, Generic
“Tree give Wolfgang sad feelings.”
–Wolfgang, Generic
“It is touched by darkness.”
–Wendy, Generic
“IT DISGUSTS ME IN MULTIPLE WAYS”
–WX-78, Generic
“Could the sense of unease I feel be a part of its defense mechanism?”
–Wickerbottom, Generic
“It's just as spooky in the day.”
–Woodie, Generic
“It provides a lovely ambiance of dread, don't you think?”
–Maxwell, Generic
“The sight öf it döth send a terrible chill döwn my spine.”
–Wigfrid, Generic
“It looks scary, but that doesn't mean it's bad!”
–Webber, Generic
“Not exactly a friendly-looking tree, non?”
–Warly, Generic
“Pretty hair, friend”
–Wormwood, Generic
“That is one gloomy tree.”
–Winona, Generic
“Is it so scary for a tree to be hairy?”
–Wortox, Generic
“Nice horns, florp!”
–Wurt, Generic
“It looks kind of like a monster... but I think it's just a tree.”
–Walter, Generic
“What a terribly off-putting tree.”
–Wanda, Generic
“It's a stump.”
–Wilson, Stump
“It made an ugly stump too.”
–Willow, Stump
“Much better.”
–Wolfgang, Stump
“This tree was put to rest.”
–Wendy, Stump
“STATE: OPTIMAL”
–WX-78, Stump
“As I thought, the sense of unease has passed.”
–Wickerbottom, Stump
“Good riddance, spooky tree.”
–Woodie, Stump
“A pity.”
–Maxwell, Stump
“Perhaps 'tis för the best.”
–Wigfrid, Stump
“Poor Mr. Tree.”
–Webber, Stump
“Nothing but a stump left.”
–Warly, Stump
“Will miss friend”
–Wormwood, Stump
“Can't say I'm sad to see it gone.”
–Winona, Stump
“It's leafy life was cut short.”
–Wortox, Stump
“Glorp. Horn tree gone.”
–Wurt, Stump
“Aww, I liked that tree...”
–Walter, Stump
“Good riddance.”
–Wanda, Stump
“It's growing! I think?”
–Wilson, Sapling
“It's almost a tree.”
–Willow, Sapling
“Little tree, grow mighty like Wolfgang!”
–Wolfgang, Sapling
“Maybe if you're lucky, you'll get the chance to grow up.”
–Wendy, Sapling
“CONDITIONS: OPTIMAL. WHO DID THIS?”
–WX-78, Sapling
“It appears to be growing nicely.”
–Wickerbottom, Sapling
“Soon, Lucy. Soon.”
–Woodie, Sapling
“It's coming along nicely.”
–Maxwell, Sapling
“These lands seem tö suit it well.”
–Wigfrid, Sapling
“We think it likes its new home.”
–Webber, Sapling
“Just a petite bébé.”
–Warly, Sapling
“Happy young friend!”
–Wormwood, Sapling
“Looks like it's on its way.”
–Winona, Sapling
“Not to get sappy, I'm glad it's happy.”
–Wortox, Sapling
“Plant's friend growing!”
–Wurt, Sapling
“I can't wait to see what kind of tree it grows into!”
–Walter, Sapling
“Only marginally more exciting than watching grass grow.”
–Wanda, Sapling
“I need to plant this in the right place.”
–Wilson, Sapling (Item)
“I bet you want some dirt, don't ya?”
–Willow, Sapling (Item)
“Don't worry, little tree. Wolfgang find good home for you.”
–Wolfgang, Sapling (Item)
“Don't worry. I'll bury you.”
–Wendy, Sapling (Item)
“WHERE SHOULD I PLANT FILTY ORGANIC TO EVENTUALLY DIE?”
–WX-78, Sapling (Item)
“I will endeavor to find a suitable environment for it.”
–Wickerbottom, Sapling (Item)
“Where would you like to be chopped down eventually?”
–Woodie, Sapling (Item)
“I suppose I have no choice but to get my hands dirty.”
–Maxwell, Sapling (Item)
“Fear nöt, I shall find yöu a place tö lay döwn yöur röots.”
–Wigfrid, Sapling (Item)
“We're going to find a good home for you!”
–Webber, Sapling (Item)
“Allons-y! I can't just leave this little tree uprooted!”
–Warly, Sapling (Item)
“Where young friend want to live?”
–Wormwood, Sapling (Item)
“This might be one of them finicky trees.”
–Winona, Sapling (Item)
“It's tricky with a tree so picky.”
–Wortox, Sapling (Item)
“Where put Plant's friend?”
–Wurt, Sapling (Item)
“Sniff out a good spot for us to plant the tree, Woby!”
–Walter, Sapling (Item)
“Now, if I could only remember what type of soil it likes...”
–Wanda, Sapling (Item)

Tips[edit | edit source]
Maxwell's Shadow Servants will not mine or harvest the Gloomthorns.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
Gloomthorn concept art from Staying Afloat update stream from Rhymes with Play.
Gloomthorn concept art from Staying Afloat update stream from Rhymes with Play.

The Nightberries can only be picked at night. The timer for Nightberry regrowth is set upon creation of the seed and will stay the same throughout its lifetime and every harvest. Chopping the tree and replanting the seed will not change this regrowth timer. The regrowth timer will be reduced by 25% in [[Spring]], despite the produce being edible.
